data.title: SoftBank Vision Fund plans to lay off 20% of staff as Masayoshi Son shifts strategic focus to AI
data.description: SoftBank Group's Vision Fund is considering cutting up to 20% of its staff, according to people familiar with the matter, marking a strategic shift by Masayoshi Son to bold bets in the field of artificial intelligence. The person said that the Vision Fund, which had a total of about 282 employees as of the end of March, may cut more than 50 positions. As Masayoshi Son's interest in large-scale artificial intelligence bets has grown, the importance of the Vision Fund department has gradually diminished, and the layoffs continue years of reductions. A spokesperson for the Vision Fund confirmed the upcoming layoffs but did not disclose specific details. "We continue to adjust our organizational structure to better execute our long-term strategy of making bold and determined investments in AI and breakthrough technologies," the spokesperson said in an email. SoftBank Vision Fund has been selling key assets to raise funds for large-scale AI investments. As the company focuses on a few large investment transactions, the Vision Fund no longer needs a large number of investment advisors to manage new or existing transactions. (Jinshi)

data.title: Ethereum's Staking Withdrawal Queue Sparks Debate Among Experts
data.description:
According to PANews, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin recently addressed concerns regarding the extended staking withdrawal queue, which has now surpassed six weeks. On September 18, Buterin shared his thoughts on the X platform, emphasizing that this mechanism is a deliberate design choice rather than a flaw, likening it to military discipline.

Buterin highlighted that staking is a commitment to safeguarding the network, and from this perspective, the withdrawal delay serves as a security measure. He explained, "If anyone in the military could suddenly leave at any time, the army would lose cohesion," underscoring that Ethereum's reliability hinges on ensuring validators cannot abruptly abandon their duties.

However, Buterin acknowledged that the current design is not perfect. He stated, "This does not mean the current staking queue design is the optimal solution, but blindly lowering the threshold would significantly reduce the chain's credibility for nodes that are not frequently online."

Buterin's views align with those of EigenLayer founder Sreeram Kannan. On September 17, Kannan described Ethereum's lengthy withdrawal period as a "conservative parameter," deeming it a crucial security measure. He explained that the waiting time effectively prevents worst-case scenarios, such as coordinated validator attacks, where participants might attempt to exit collectively before facing penalties.

Kannan warned, "Unstaking should never be instantaneous." He further elaborated that shortening the process to a few days could expose Ethereum to attacks that deplete its security assumptions. Conversely, a longer window allows for the detection and punishment of malicious activities like double-signing, ensuring that rogue validators cannot easily evade accountability.

Kannan specifically noted that this buffer mechanism enables inactive nodes to reconnect and periodically verify the correct fork. He stressed that without this mechanism, competing forks might claim legitimacy, making it difficult for offline nodes to discern authenticity upon reconnecting.

He concluded, "Ethereum does not employ a fixed long-term unstaking mechanism but is designed to handle instant processing when a small amount of staking exits during specific periods. However, if a large amount of staking simultaneously requests withdrawal, the queue accumulates, potentially extending to several months in the worst-case scenario."

This robust defense comes as Ethereum's withdrawal queue reaches a historic high. Data from the Ethereum validator queue indicates that the current withdrawal backlog has reached 43 days, involving 2.48 million ETH (approximately $11.3 billion) awaiting extraction.

data.title: A whale deposited $3 million USDC into HyperLiquid and opened long positions on BONK, ETHFI, PENGU, and other tokens.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale deposited $3 million USDC into HyperLiquid and opened long positions in BONK, ETHFI, PENGU, and PEPE with 1x leverage.

data.title: Jobless breaks through 0.016 USDT, rising over 400% intraday
data.description: According to GMGN data, the price of Jobless briefly surpassed 0.016 USDT, pushing its market capitalization above $16 million. Currently trading at $16.3 million, it represents a daily increase of over 400%. Reportedly, thanks to the recent surge in BNB prices above $1,000, some BNB holders have begun promoting the "hold BNB, be like a jobless person" lifestyle, a similar concept to the meme coin. Odaily reminds users that meme coins are subject to significant price fluctuations, so investors should exercise caution.

data.title: Bank of Japan Maintains Interest Rate at 0.5%
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the Bank of Japan has decided to keep its benchmark interest rate unchanged at 0.5% for the fifth consecutive meeting. This decision aligns with market expectations.

data.title: The Ethereum mainnet Fusaka upgrade is tentatively scheduled for December 3rd this year.
data.description: Christine Kim, former Vice President of Research at Galaxy Digital, posted on the X platform that important decisions were made during today's Ethereum developer call, ACDC #165. Developers confirmed the public testnet schedule and the Fusaka BPO hard fork. The Fusaka mainnet upgrade is scheduled for Epoch 411392 on December 3rd of this year. Fusaka is the next major hard fork after the Pectra upgrade in May 2025. Focused on network scalability, efficiency, and resilience, it forms a core part of Ethereum's "Surge" phase, aiming to reduce Layer 2 (L2) rollup fees and increase overall throughput by optimizing data availability and validator responsibilities. Preliminary analysis of Fusaka Devnet-5 suggests that blob capacity should more than double within two weeks of its launch.

data.title: Bullish, a NYSE-listed company, officially released its financial report showcasing Pudgy Penguins and PENGU
data.description: NYSE-listed cryptocurrency exchange Bullish (NYSE: BLSH) officially unveiled Pudgy Penguins and $PENGU in its Q2 earnings report, marking the Web 3 brand's first Wall Street debut to institutional investors. During the earnings call, a slideshow showcasing PENGU data was presented to major financial institutions and analysts, signaling institutional recognition for the digital asset.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Revises UK Interest Rate Forecast
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Goldman Sachs has revised its forecast regarding the Bank of England's interest rate policy. The financial institution no longer anticipates a rate cut in 2025, which was previously expected in November. Instead, Goldman Sachs now predicts that the Bank of England will announce its next rate cut in February, followed by quarterly reductions. The terminal rate is projected to reach 3% by the end of 2026.

data.title: Rainfall joins Aethir EcoDrop program
data.description: Aethir announced on the X platform that Rainfall has joined Aethir's EcoDrop program and will provide a series of benefits to the community. Users can visit its official website to claim their rewards.

data.title: The crypto market rose for three consecutive days, with the GameFi sector rising by more than 5% and BTC breaking through $117,000.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the crypto market saw three consecutive days of gains. The GameFi sector saw a 24-hour gain of 5.45%. Within the sector, ImmutableX (IMX) surged 26.32%, while GALA and Beam (BEAM) rose 3.74% and 9.14%, respectively. Additionally, Bitcoin (BTC) rose 0.37%, breaking through $117,000, while Ethereum (ETH) fell 0.40%, fluctuating in a narrow range around $4,600. Other sectors that performed well included: the Layer 2 sector, which rose 4.71% over the past 24 hours. Within this sector, Optimism (OP) and Mantle (MNT) rose 3.93% and 6.33%, respectively. The NFT sector rose 2.35%, with Pudgy Penguins (PENGU) up 3.42%. The DeFi sector rose 1.31%, with Chainlink (LINK) up 3.25%. The Layer 1 sector rose 0.70%, with Avalanche (AVAX) up 9.14%. Among other sectors, the PayFi sector fell 0.20%, but Trust Wallet (TWT) bucked the trend and rose 19.13%. The CeFi sector fell 0.30%, while ApolloX (APX) rose 30.73%. The Meme sector fell 1.43%, with previously high-performing Pump.fun (PUMP) and MemeCore (M) dropping 10.80% and 12.06%, respectively. The crypto sector index, which reflects the historical market trends of the sector, shows that the ssiGameFi, ssiLayer 2, and ssiNFT indices rose by 9.28%, 5.36%, and 3.30%, respectively.

data.title: A whale is suspected of selling 20.92 million APX on Aster, worth $12.3 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, a whale named "0xD3DA...8c41" appears to have sold 20.92 million APX on Aster, currently valued at $12.3 million. The whale deposited 20.92 million APX into Aster two days ago and subsequently withdrew 10.75 million USDT from Aster, selling the tokens at an average price of $0.514.

data.title: Bitcoin billionaires Tyler and Cameron Winklevoss torpedo White House pick in crypto power move
data.description:
Tyler and Cameron Winklevoss, both billionaires and longtime crypto backers of President Trump, are openly fighting Brian Quintenz, the man Trump nominated to run the Commodity Futures Trading Commission. The clash broke into public view last week as the twins prepared their company Gemini Space Station for its market debut, as Cryptopolitan reported earlier. The […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/tyler-cameron-winklevoss-crypto-power-move/

data.title: STBL responds to rumors of "market maker selling": No abnormal selling activity was observed, and related transactions may be user arbitrage behavior
data.description: STBL officially issued a statement on X clarifying some controversial discussions during the TGE. STBL stated that recent social media speculation regarding market makers selling tokens during the TGE has led to no unusual selling activity observed, beyond normal liquidity provision in a rising market. Regarding speculation regarding addresses and activities related to market maker Auros Global, while some token supply did originate from the market maker, detailed verification has confirmed these speculations to be unfounded. The wallet address 0x7654D09b4Ead2eB6119a974c74071942Cc56866C is not affiliated with Auros or STBL and is actually an exchange hot wallet. On-chain transfers that some observers mistakenly identified as sales were actually operational transactions to the exchange's wallet. Given the high market demand during the exchange's initial public offering, it is speculated that these activities may have been arbitrage attempts by users exploiting the price difference between the exchange and the on-chain platform. It should be emphasized that these trading intentions are merely speculations based on market phenomena, and neither STBL nor Auros engages in any arbitrage price behavior.

data.title: Pantera: Bitcoin and Ethereum’s narratives are set, Solana has greater upside potential
data.description: Pantera Capital officially announced on X that Solana is reaching a critical inflection point in consumer, fintech, and institutional adoption. Blue-chip companies like Stripe and PayPal have already begun building applications on it. While the narratives for Bitcoin and Ethereum are largely established, Solana's is just beginning to unfold. Solana is still in its early stages. Currently, there is no Solana ETF, and only five publicly traded companies hold Solana. Institutional allocations to Solana are significantly undervalued relative to BTC and ETH, holding less than 1% of the total supply (BTC is 16% and ETH is 7%). In terms of market capitalization, Solana is still only 1/20th that of Bitcoin and 1/4th that of Ethereum—but it already leads both in key usage metrics. Solana's adoption narrative is just beginning, offering greater asymmetric upside potential.

data.title: A whale bought another 155,000 UNI and 16,600 LINK
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a whale (0x4c64...015A) bought another 155,455 UNI (worth $1.51 million) and 16,606 LINK (worth $412,000) 10 minutes ago. The whale currently holds a total of 1.13 million UNI (worth $11 million) and 74,281 LINK (worth $1.84 million).

data.title: Altcoin Season Index Indicates Strong Altcoin Performance
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the Altcoin Season Index, as reported by Coinmarketcap, stands at 76 today, slightly down from 77 yesterday. This marks two consecutive days in the 'Altcoin Season' zone, surpassing last week's average of 68 and last month's average of 46. The index reached its highest point in the past year at 87 last December.

The Altcoin Season Index reflects that over the past 90 days, approximately 70 out of the top 100 cryptocurrencies by market capitalization have outperformed Bitcoin.

data.title: NBA star Kevin Durant has regained access to his Coinbase account
data.description: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong posted on the X platform that NBA star Kevin Durant has regained access to his Coinbase account.

data.title: Solana's Adoption Reaches Crucial Turning Point
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Pantera Capital recently highlighted a significant turning point in the adoption of Solana by consumers, fintech companies, and institutions. Major firms like Stripe and PayPal have begun developing applications on the Solana platform. While the narratives for Bitcoin and Ethereum are well-established, Solana's story is just beginning to unfold.

Currently, Solana is in its early stages, with no SOL ETFs available and only five publicly traded companies holding SOL. Institutional allocation of SOL is significantly lower compared to Bitcoin and Ethereum, with less than 1% of the total supply held by institutions, in contrast to 16% for Bitcoin and 7% for Ethereum. In terms of market capitalization, Solana is valued at only one-twentieth of Bitcoin and one-fourth of Ethereum. However, Solana is already leading in key usage metrics, indicating a narrative of adoption that is just starting, with a potential for asymmetric growth.

data.title: Consensys founder: MetaMask will issue coins, and it will be faster than you expect
data.description: “The MetaMask token is coming, and it may be coming sooner than you expect,” Joseph Rubin, founder of MetaMask’s parent company ConsenSys, said in an interview with The Crypto Beat, a podcast published by The Block.
